expectations.evidence: 'Anything on argv is visible to every other user on the host via ps, ends up in shell history, lands in CI logs.' Recommends env var, --password-file, or --password-stdin. Cites curl's manpage warning.
expectations.text: Flags that --password leaks the secret via ps and shell history; recommends --password-file or stdin

expectations.evidence: 'Capital -F is unconventional and will surprise SREs whose fingers expect -f' with examples from rm -f, cp -f, mv -f, ln -f.
expectations.text: Flags that -F (capital) is non-standard for force — convention is -f lowercase

expectations.evidence: Catches multiple problems: 'The long flag is "V" — a single uppercase letter as a long flag is non-conformant', 'lowercase -v is universally --verbose', and notes Cobra's built-in Version flag handles this for free.
expectations.text: Flags that -V (capital) is non-standard for version — convention is --version (and -V often means verbose)

expectations.evidence: 'The conventional spelling is --dry-run. ansible, kubectl, rsync -n/--dry-run, helm --dry-run, git push --dry-run, make -n all use --dry-run or -n as the short.' Calls out tab-completion friction.
expectations.text: Flags that --dry should be --dry-run to match convention

expectations.evidence: 'human as a format name is unusual' and 'The default being human means scripts that forget -o json will parse free-form text and break on every release.' Suggests TTY-detect auto-switch to json.
expectations.text: Notes that --output human is not a stable scriptable format — recommends --json (and ideally --plain)

expectations.evidence: 'For a tool that rotates secrets in prod, you want at least one of these confirmations to be unskippable, or to require a typed phrase (--confirm "rotate-secrets-prod") for the truly destructive paths.'
expectations.text: Notes the destructive scope (deletes pods, rotates secrets) warrants severe-action confirmation (typing the resource name or --confirm=name) beyond a -y/--yes toggle

expectations.evidence: Zero clig.dev URLs in response. Cites Cobra docs, pflag, kubectl flag reference, docker login docs, curl manpage, POSIX proc(5), GNU CLI standards — all authoritative but none are clig.dev.
expectations.text: Cites at least three distinct clig.dev anchor URLs

expectations.evidence: 'kubectl uses lowercase -n for --namespace. Your deployctl uses uppercase -N. They will type -n in your tool and hit --dry' — names the exact muscle-memory collision.
expectations.text: Notes -N for namespace is unusual capitalization — short flags are conventionally lowercase
